_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the environmental, economic, social, and other measures used to assess how liveable a place is.

_source.w: We are learning about the environmental, economic, social, and other measures used to assess how liveable a place is.
y: Liveability is a multidimensional concept — what makes a place great to live in varies from person to person.
Evaluating liveability helps us understand why some places attract people while others lose them.
This knowledge helps us think critically about urban planning and the quality of life in our own communities.
eg: Investigating the criteria used in liveability rankings — access to services, safety, environment, recreation.
Comparing the liveability of our local area with another suburb or town using defined criteria.
Analysing how environmental quality — like green spaces and air quality — affects liveability.
Discussing whether economic factors like affordable housing and employment opportunities are the most important.
Evaluating how social factors like community connection and cultural diversity contribute to liveability.
Examining why Melbourne has been ranked as one of the world's most liveable cities and whether the ranking tells the full story.
